text.insert: The code component owns a single block type,
— the language-tagged block for real, annotated source listings in the
. The source lives in the block's delta text; the language tag and structured line annotations live in
In the documentation doctrine the type is the source-evidence surface:
blocks carry state examples, code blocks carry the evidence — annotated listings of the defining source.
A live instance of the type — a small annotated listing:

annotations.label: Guard
annotations.note: Rejects a document whose root pointer names no block — the tree invariant every reader assumes.
Callers get the resolved root shell, never the raw id.

text.insert: : the delta text is the source payload itself, not prose. The editor schema sets
), so the spans are plain inserts — no bold, link, or reference marks inside code. Everything else is props, defined by two closed TypeBox schemas:

annotations.label: Annotation shape
annotations.note: lines and note are required strings, label optional. Closed object: an unknown key is a validation refusal, not a pass-through.

annotations.label: Block props
annotations.note: Both props optional — a bare code block is valid. Every write revalidates the array items against the annotation shape.

annotations.label: Text carrier
annotations.note: carriesText: true — the one non-prose text carrier in the vocabulary.

rows: Fence tag for highlighting and the header label, e.g. "json", "ts". Written by the editor's language picker.
Array of { lines, label?, note }: lines is a 1-indexed range string and the entry's identity key, label an optional short heading on the note, note the annotation body.

text.insert: is a 1-indexed range string: "4" covers one line, "4-9" a span, "1,4-6" a comma list.
The exact string is the annotation's identity key.
Both typed actions and the pairing engine key on it; two annotations can never share a key —
expands a key into the covered line set.
Parts clamp to the text's line count; unparseable parts contribute nothing — bad input never crashes a render.
Two tolerant readers, one per package.
) feeds every doc surface, so edit and read mode can never disagree about which entries are renderable.
Both skip entries missing a non-empty
returns null when nothing renderable remains, so a surface branches on "has annotations at all" with one check.
The component registers exactly two typed actions, both built with
. Annotations edit only through them; the source text stays on generic text ops — no typed action touches the delta.
string: replaces in place when the key exists, appends otherwise.
omitted from params stays omitted in the stored entry.
Refuses a key that does not exist — the failure reports issue path

operations.name: code.setAnnotation
operations.description: Upsert a line annotation keyed by its exact "lines" string (e.g. "4-9").

operations.name: code.removeAnnotation
operations.description: Remove the annotation whose "lines" key matches exactly.

annotations.label: Tolerant read
annotations.note: Malformed stored entries drop before the upsert — one bad entry never wedges the block.

annotations.label: Exact-key upsert
annotations.note: The lines string is the identity: replace when it exists, append when it does not.

annotations.label: Props patch
annotations.note: A shallow-merge patch replacing the whole annotations array; executing it is the adapter's job.

text.insert: Three surfaces, one shell: the plain read surface (
). Shared furniture — header row, sticky gutter, zebra striping, notes aside — comes from
, so the three surfaces look identical.
Quiet uppercase language label left, ghost copy button right — no pill, no background.
The copy button appears on block hover or focus and copies the surface's exact displayed source.
A sticky 3rem column that stays put under horizontal scroll; numbers render at 55% strength at rest.
Its background must be opaque (it is sticky) but defaults to a mix matching the block background, so no band is perceptible.
One absolute layer behind the code column; the gradient period is two 20px lines, aligned to line 1 by construction.
A 320px right column at lg widths, stacked below the code when narrow.
Notes are plain prose rows with a hairline rule between items — never zebra; each note's
key rides in its title attribute.
Design principle: one surface, one accent, interaction reveals the rest.
At rest an annotated range shows only the 2px accent bar plus accent line numbers; the tint appears when its pair is lit.
is a layout constant, not a theme token.
The zebra gradient period, the gutter row height, and every annotation overlay's top and height are computed from it, so it must never drift per host.
Soft wrap is off on every surface.
on its content node; wrapping would break every line-geometry computation.
The annotated read surface keeps a per-line grid, so every annotated line is a click and focus target.
Pairing runs on the shared LinkGroup engine — one group per block, keyed by the annotation's
Hovering or focusing a note or an annotated line lights the pair's full extent: background wash, a 3px pin rail from first through last covered line, pin-color bold numbers.
Clicking (or Enter/Space) pins the pair — it survives hover-out — and Escape clears it.
Overlapping annotations resolve each line to the earliest covering note.
and the read surface share the rule, so overlay geometry and click-to-pair can never disagree.
The edit surface pairs from the notes side only.
Clicking a note sticky-toggles its pair and scrolls the range's first line into view; clicks in the code just place the cursor.
): the paragraph converts the moment the third backtick lands — no trailing space, no typed language tag.
The header label is the language picker.
Choosing a language writes the block's
"auto" clears it and falls back to detection, showing the sniffed language when one resolves.
) uses highlight.js core with a curated set of 13 registered grammars — never the all-languages bundle.
bash, css, diff, go, javascript, json, markdown, python, rust, sql, typescript, xml, yaml — plus each grammar's aliases (ts, tsx, js, sh, yml, md, py…).
One resolution serves the header label and the tokens.
The declared language when a grammar or alias matches, else a JSON sniff, else escaped plain text with no label — the label can never disagree with tokenization.
Token colors come from the host's
theme vars for light and dark.
JSON pretty-printing is display-only; the stored text is never mutated.
re-renders one-liner JSON as the nested 2-space form when the language is json/jsonc, or is undeclared and the text sniffs as JSON.
On the annotated surface it runs before line-splitting, so
ranges address the pretty-printed form — author JSON as pretty multi-line text so ranges are stable against the transform.
) the block renders as a fenced markdown block: the fence tag is
(a bare fence when unset), the body is the delta text as plain text. Annotations follow the fence, one blockquote line per entry:
The L-prefixed range is the annotation's
key; the label rides in parentheses and drops cleanly when absent.
string drop from the projection — the same tolerant read as the doc surfaces.
are 1-indexed against the block text at the time you write them; re-check them after editing the source.
The house style for documenting a system: a
carrying both the shape and an example instance, then an
for the operations. Code blocks enter as source evidence — annotated listings of the defining source, not state examples.

rows: Language-picker affordance on block hover (edit surface)
Annotation accent: gutter bar, accent numbers, lit tint
Gutter numbers and the copy button
Sticky gutter background (defaults to a mix matching the block bg)
Internal hairlines: header rule, column divider, note dividers

text.insert: The three knobs are the registry's only non-color code tokens.
Every internal hairline — header rule, code/notes column divider, note dividers — runs through the one rule token set.
